parts.title: Reception class teacher
prompts: Write a short guide for introducing a new [specific skill, e.g. writing their name] to a reception class (6-year-olds), with three steps and a playful method.
Suggest three outdoor activities for a reception class (6-year-olds) linked to [specific theme, e.g. nature], with one activity for each and minimal materials.
Give me five strategies for supporting a shy 6-year-old to participate in a [specific activity, e.g. morning circle], with one simple and reassuring idea for each.
Create a list of five short encouraging phrases to build a 6-year-old's self-confidence during a [specific activity, e.g. counting], in the reception class.

Create a day plan for a reception class (6-year-olds) that integrates play and learning in line with [reference to current curriculum], with three activities linked to [specific goal, e.g. letter recognition], and a short reflection.
Give me an example of a routine for starting the day in reception class (6-year-olds), with three steps and a simple [specific activity, e.g. morning song] that prepares children for the school day.

Suggest three ways to use everyday materials (e.g. [specific material, e.g. building blocks]) with a reception class (6-year-olds) to practise [specific goal, e.g. mathematics], with one idea for each.
Create a plan for a reception class (6-year-olds) to practise [specific school routine, e.g. lining up], with three stages (introduction, practice, reflection).
Give me five ideas for playful activities in reception class (6-year-olds) that develop [specific goal, e.g. fine motor skills], with a short description for each.

Write a short template for documenting children's learning of [specific goal, e.g. letter sounds] in a reception class (6-year-olds), with three observation points in line with [reference to current curriculum].
Suggest three ways to collaborate with grade 1 teachers to ease the transition for a reception class (6-year-olds), focusing on [specific goal, e.g. reading], with one idea for each.
Give me an example of an outdoor activity for a reception class (6-year-olds) that introduces [specific unit, e.g. counting], with three steps and a reflection.
Create a list of five simple creative activities for a reception class (6-year-olds) linked to [specific theme, e.g. spring], with a short description for each.
Suggest three ways to use stories with a reception class (6-year-olds) to practise [specific goal, e.g. listening], with one story idea for each and a simple follow-up.
Write a short guide for organising a [specific activity, e.g. movement game] for a reception class (6-year-olds), with three steps and a focus on [specific goal, e.g. cooperation].
Give me five ideas for calm activities in reception class (6-year-olds) that prepare children for [specific goal, e.g. concentration], with one activity for each and minimal resources.
Suggest three ways to use the school grounds with a reception class (6-year-olds) to explore [specific theme, e.g. weather], with one hands-on task for each.
Create a plan for a reception class (6-year-olds) to practise [specific school routine, e.g. packing their bag], with three steps and a playful method.
Give me an example of a group activity for a reception class (6-year-olds) where they play a [specific game, e.g. memory], with three steps and a focus on [specific goal, e.g. memory].
Create an activity for a reception class (6-year-olds) where they explore [specific theme, e.g. letters] through play, with three steps and a simple reflection.
Give me five ways to use nature with a reception class (6-year-olds) to practise [specific goal, e.g. counting], with one playful idea for each.
Write a short guide for introducing a [specific school routine, e.g. morning circle] to a reception class (6-year-olds), with three steps and a song or rhyme.
Suggest three ways to use group activities with a reception class (6-year-olds) to practise [specific goal, e.g. listening], with one game for each.
Create a list of five simple outdoor activities for a reception class (6-year-olds) linked to [specific theme, e.g. shapes], with a description for each.
Give me an example of a routine for ending the day in reception class (6-year-olds), with three steps and a calm [specific activity, e.g. story time].
Suggest three ways to use simple materials (e.g. [specific material, e.g. paper]) with a reception class (6-year-olds) to practise [specific goal, e.g. writing], with one idea for each.
Write a short template for a reception class teacher to plan a [specific activity, e.g. letter hunt] for 6-year-olds, with three stages (introduction, activity, reflection).
Give me five ideas for activities in reception class (6-year-olds) linked to [specific theme, e.g. friendship], with one activity for each and simple instructions.
Create a plan for a reception class (6-year-olds) where they explore [specific theme, e.g. numbers] through play, with three steps and a simple follow-up.
Suggest three ways to collaborate with after-school staff for a reception class (6-year-olds) to practise [specific goal, e.g. cooperation], with one idea for each.
Give me an example of a simple outdoor activity for a reception class (6-year-olds) that practises [specific goal, e.g. counting], with three steps.
Write a list of five theme days for a reception class (6-year-olds) linked to [specific theme, e.g. nature], with a short activity description for each.
Suggest three ways to use the reception class outdoor area with a group (6-year-olds) to learn about [specific theme, e.g. weather], with one task for each.
Create a plan for a reception class teacher to lead a [specific activity, e.g. singing game] for 6-year-olds, with three steps and a focus on [specific goal, e.g. rhythm].
Give me five ideas for indoor activities for a reception class (6-year-olds) that develop [specific goal, e.g. letter recognition], with one activity for each.
Suggest three ways to use movement with a reception class (6-year-olds) to strengthen [specific goal, e.g. coordination], with one idea for each.
Write a short guide for introducing a [specific game, e.g. 'Follow the Leader'] to a reception class (6-year-olds), with three steps and a theme such as [specific theme, e.g. movement].
Give me an example of an activity for a reception class (6-year-olds) where they practise [specific skill, e.g. listening] through stories, with three steps.

Create a list of three short ways to use nature to teach a reception class (6-year-olds) about [specific theme, e.g. numbers], with one suggestion for each and a link to [reference to current curriculum].
Suggest three fun movement activities for a reception class (6-year-olds) that work as movement breaks when children are finding it hard to sit still.
